pr.title: table-typer: Fill in lots of StageCallback types
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Large table-typer pass over stage/ground code that replaced many StageCallbacks-related UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete callback signatures, primarily void or bool functions taking Ground_GObj*. Matching output was unchanged according to the decomp.dev bot. The author noted this was not the normal table-typer workflow because many StageCallbacks tables were still asm-only; instead, a custom run walked Ground_803DFEDC entries, selected their second element as a StageCallbacks table, and applied the usual replacement script to those tables.
what_changed: Filled in many StageCallbacks function prototypes across 42 changed files in src/melee/gr, replacing placeholder UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete signatures.

Updated corresponding stub or decompiled function definitions so callbacks now accept Ground_GObj* instead of void, for example bool grBigBlue_801E5B10(Ground_GObj* arg) and void grBigBlue_801E5B18(Ground_GObj* arg).

Standardized callback return types seen in StageCallbacks tables: many callbacks became void Ground_GObj* handlers, while predicate/update-style callbacks became bool Ground_GObj* functions returning false in existing stubs.
Added #include "it/inlines.h" to multiple ground headers where the newly exposed Ground_GObj* parameter type was needed.

Touched many stage families, including Big Blue, Big Blue Route, Castle, Corneria, Fourside, Garden, Greens, Home Run, Kongo, Mute City, Old Kongo, Old Pupupu, Old Yoshi, Push On, Rainbow Cruise, Shrine Route, Venom, Zebes, and Zebes Route.
Updated tools/table-typer/go.sum with two added dependency checksum lines.
smart_moves: Used table structure as evidence for typing: the custom pass read Ground_803DFEDC, then followed each entry's second element as a StageCallbacks table before applying the usual replacement script.

Kept the bespoke traversal out of the generic tool because the author judged it too specific; the PR body explicitly says the better long-term route is translating StageCallbacks from asm to C and then running table-typer normally.
Made broad mechanical header/source updates while preserving binary matching; the bot report says GALE01 had no changes.
Propagated signatures into both declarations and definitions, avoiding header/source disagreement for callbacks that were already represented in C stubs.
decomp_lessons: For callback tables, table membership can reveal function signatures even before every referenced table has been translated to C.
A StageCallbacks function in this pass commonly has a Ground_GObj* parameter; future ground/stage callback typing should check table context before leaving declarations as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
When a tool assumes C-visible table definitions but the real evidence is still asm-only, a one-off extraction can unblock type propagation, but the reusable fix is to migrate the tables into C first.
Large mechanical type passes should include both prototype and implementation edits and rely on match/no-change reports to verify that parameter-name additions did not affect codegen.
Adding a concrete pointer type to public headers may require include cleanup; this PR added it/inlines.h in several headers to support Ground_GObj* usage.
naming_conventions: Existing address-based ground symbol names were preserved, such as grBigBlue_801E5B10, grCastle_801CD600, and grCorneria_801DD64C.

Anonymous callback parameters in prototypes are written as Ground_GObj* without a name, while source definitions commonly used Ground_GObj* arg unless an existing meaningful name like gobj or ground_gobj was already present.
Boolean callback definitions retained bool return types and existing simple stub bodies such as return false.
Stage-specific prefix capitalization and existing gr* module naming were not renamed in this PR; the work was type propagation, not symbol recovery.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Custom table-typer run handled asm-only StageCallbacks tables by discovering them indirectly through Ground_803DFEDC entries.
The replacement script was applied to the discovered StageCallbacks tables rather than requiring normal .h/.c definitions.
Function parameters were added to empty or simple stubs without changing behavior, relying on unused-parameter compatibility for matching.
decomp.dev reported 'No changes' for GALE01, giving evidence that the signature and include updates were non-codegen-changing.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only issue comment was the decomp.dev bot report: 'Report for GALE01 ... No changes.'
Author self-feedback: this was not the normal table-typer path, and the long-term better approach is to translate all StageCallbacks from asm to C before running the tool normally.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining StageCallbacks tables still asm-only after PR 1803.
Search for StageCallbacks declarations still using UNK_RET or UNK_PARAMS.
Audit ground headers that include it/inlines.h only to expose Ground_GObj* and consider a narrower forward declaration if available.
Run table-typer normally after translating StageCallbacks tables from asm to C.
Compare callback signatures inferred from Ground_803DFEDC against current StageCallbacks struct definitions.
